name: Ruta azul Azarbes de Dalt y Convenio  — Parque Natural El Hondo — ida y vuelta
region_intro: Family friendly hiking trails in El Fondó/El Hondo are primarily located within a significant natural wetland and protected Natural Park. This expansive area, situated between Elche and Crevillent, is characterized by a complex system of reservoirs, lagoons, and vast reed beds. The terrain is largely flat, offering accessible and easy-to-navigate paths suitable for various fitness levels. Beyond the wetlands, the region also features palm forests and cultivated lands, contributing to its diverse scenery. Best family friendly hiking trails in El Fondó/El Hondo The most popular family friendly hiking route is Green Route: El Rincón and El Saladar Lagoons, a 2.7 miles (4.4 km) trail that takes 1 hour 5 minutes to complete. This easy circular route offers panoramic lagoon views and excellent birdwatching opportunities. Another top favourite among local hikers is Wooden footbridge – Birdwatching observatory loop from Centro de interpretación Parque Natural el Fondo, an easy 2.5 miles (4.1 km) path. This trail provides a short, inclusive experience around the information center, often featuring wooden walkways. Local hikers also love the Birdwatching observatory – Bassa Illarguera Viewpoint loop from Casa de los toros, a 6.2 miles (10.0 km) trail leading through the park's wetland landscape, often completed in about 2 hours 30 minutes. Hiking in El Fondó/El Hondo is defined by extensive wetlands, lagoons, and reed beds, with strategically placed bird observatories. The network offers options for different ability levels, from short, easy walks to longer routes. The routes in El Fondó/El Hondo are highly rated by the komoot community with an average score of 4.3 stars from over 600 reviews. More than 3,600 hikers have used komoot to explore El Fondó/El Hondo's varied terrain.
region_faq:
Q: How many family-friendly hiking trails are available in El Fondó/El Hondo?
A: El Fondó/El Hondo offers a wide variety of trails suitable for families, with over 100 routes to explore. More than 50 of these are specifically rated as easy, making them perfect for all ages and skill levels.

Q: What makes El Fondó/El Hondo a good destination for family hikes?
A: The region is characterized by its largely flat terrain and well-maintained paths, making it very accessible for families with children or strollers. As a significant natural wetland and protected Natural Park, it offers unique landscapes and abundant wildlife, especially birds, providing an engaging experience for young explorers.

Q: Are there any easy, circular routes suitable for families?
A: Yes, there are several easy, circular routes. A great option is the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1316845595/green-route-el-rincon-and-el-saladar-lagoons" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Green Route: El Rincón and El Saladar Lagoons&lt;/a&gt;, which is about 4.3 kilometers long and offers excellent birdwatching opportunities. Another easy loop is the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/27633545" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Wooden footbridge – Birdwatching observatory loop from Centro de interpretación Parque Natural el Fondo&lt;/a&gt;, spanning about 4 kilometers.

Q: What kind of wildlife can we expect to see on family hikes?
A: El Fondó/El Hondo is a paradise for birdwatchers, home to over 170 bird species, including rare ones like the marbled duck and white-headed duck. You might spot large flocks of flamingos, spoonbills, and various ducks and waders, especially during migration seasons. The numerous observatories and hides throughout the park provide excellent viewing opportunities without disturbing the wildlife.

Q: Are there specific viewpoints or attractions along the family trails?
A: Absolutely! The park is dotted with bird observatories that double as fantastic viewpoints. Highlights include the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/4092860" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;La Anguilla Observatory&lt;/a&gt; and the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/4036358" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Torre El Rincón Bird Observatory&lt;/a&gt;, both offering panoramic views of the lagoons and excellent spots for wildlife observation. The &lt;a href="https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/El_Fondo_Natural_Park" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;El Hondo Natural Park&lt;/a&gt; itself is a major attraction.

Q: Can we bring dogs on the family-friendly hiking trails?
A: While the Natural Park is a protected area, some trails may allow dogs on a leash. It's always best to check specific park regulations or signage at trailheads, as rules can vary to protect the sensitive wetland ecosystem and its wildlife.

Q: What is the best time of year to visit El Fondó/El Hondo for family hikes?
A: Spring and autumn are generally the most pleasant seasons for hiking, with milder temperatures and abundant bird activity during migration. Winter can also be good, especially for birdwatching. Summers can be hot, so early mornings or late afternoons are recommended if visiting during this time.

Q: Are there any trails suitable for strollers or wheelchairs?
A: Yes, the park is known for its accessible terrain. The Yellow Route, mentioned in regional research, is a short, inclusive route often on wooden walkways around the information center, specifically designed for people with limited mobility and baby strollers. Many of the park's main paths are flat and wide, making them suitable for strollers.

Q: Where can we find parking and trailhead access for family hikes?
A: Many of the popular routes, especially those starting near the visitor center, offer designated parking areas. The visitor center itself is a good starting point for several trails, including the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/27633545" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Wooden footbridge – Birdwatching observatory loop&lt;/a&gt;, and provides information on trailhead access.

Q: What do other hikers enjoy most about hiking in El Fondó/El Hondo?
A: The komoot community highly rates El Fondó/El Hondo, with an average score of 4.3 stars from over 600 ratings. Reviewers often praise the tranquil atmosphere, the incredible birdwatching opportunities, and the ease of navigation on the flat, well-maintained paths, making it a perfect escape into nature for families.

Q: Are there any longer family-friendly routes for older children?
A: For families with older children looking for a longer adventure, the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/14628378" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Birdwatching observatory – Bassa Illarguera Viewpoint loop from Centro de interpretación Parque Natural el Fondo&lt;/a&gt; offers a moderate 13.7-kilometer loop. It provides a deeper exploration of the park's wetlands and more extensive birdwatching opportunities.
tips:
It is located within El Hondo Natural Park, in the municipality of Crevillent, Alicante.
The area is near Finca El Rincón, in the Azarbe de Afuera region.
It is a birdwatching tower that offers panoramic views of the park's lagoons. Ideal for hiking and nature photography.

The La Anguila Observatory is a birdwatching point located in the wetlands near Elche, within the El Hondo Natural Park. It offers a privileged vantage point for observing local and migratory species in their natural environment. It is a peaceful spot, ideal for nature and ornithology lovers.
The Observatorio is easy to reach by bike - after rainy days, however, the trail may NOT be passable... Unfortunately, I couldn't see flamingos here...
